sourceMovie.title: The Invention of Lying
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Invention of Lying (2009) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dogma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Invention of Lying clicked for you, Dogma (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Winter's Tale
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Invention of Lying clicked for you, Winter's Tale (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Invention of Lying?
faq.answer: Try Dogma, Winter's Tale, I ♥ Huckabees—each shares DNA with The Invention of Lying while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Invention of Lying?
faq.answer: The Invention of Lying (2009) is best described as Comedy, Fantasy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Invention of Lying worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
